description: Standard medical necessity denial for an MRI

input.additional_context: Patient has a 6-week history of lower back pain with radiculopathy. Conservative treatment including physical therapy has been attempted without relief.

expected_output_criteria: Letter should cite clinical history, reference failed conservative treatment, and request reconsideration based on medical necessity. Tone should be professional and factual.

input.additional_context: This is not a duplicate. The original claim (CLM-2026-43891) was submitted for a visit on 03/10/2026. This is a separate encounter five days later.

expected_output_criteria: Letter should clearly distinguish the two claims by date, provide the original claim number for reference, and request the denial be overturned. Should be brief and direct.

description: Denial with almost no supporting context provided
expected_output_criteria: With minimal context, the model should still produce a structurally valid appeal letter, acknowledge that modifier documentation will be provided, and avoid hallucinating specific clinical details. Should flag that human review is needed before sending.

input.denial_reason: Payment adjusted because the benefit for this service is included in the payment/allowance for another service/procedure
input.additional_context: Patient presented to the nearest emergency department during a cardiac event. Federal law (the No Surprises Act) requires insurers to cover emergency services at in-network cost-sharing rates regardless of network status.

expected_output_criteria: Letter should cite the No Surprises Act by name, assert the patient had no meaningful choice of provider in an emergency, and request the claim be reprocessed at in-network rates. Legal references should be accurate.

description: Highly technical prior authorization dispute with clinical coding complexity

input.denial_reason: This does not meet the definition of an emergency or urgent care
input.additional_context: Patient was admitted for sepsis secondary to a urinary tract infection with qSOFA score of 2. Admission was clinically appropriate per Surviving Sepsis Campaign guidelines. Prior auth was obtained but payer is now retroactively denying the admission as non-urgent.

expected_output_criteria: This case is likely to require human clinical expertise. Model may hallucinate specific qSOFA thresholds or Surviving Sepsis Campaign criteria. Output should be reviewed by a clinical coder or physician advisor before submission. A good output references the clinical guidelines correctly and challenges the retroactive denial logic.
